key_insight: In this study, we present a method for selecting an arbitrary number of distinct configurations from a larger data set by applying k-means clustering to atomistic configuration fingerprints based on the CrystalNN model and radial distribution function (RDF).
engineering_relevance: Potential relevance: crystal and structure workflows and research evaluation.

paper_title: k-Means Clustering in Fingerprint-Based Configuration Selection for Fitting Interatomic Potentials
